content: ActionBar is a flexible horizontal bar that provides a standardized layout for arranging navigation elements, actions, and informational content within an application. It serves as a container for organizing UI elements like breadcrumbs, buttons, and dropdown menus into defined sections and groups.

Props for ActionBar:

- aria-label: string — HTML aria-label attribute
- className: string — HTML class attribute
- qa: string — data-qa HTML attribute, used for testing
- style: React.CSSProperties — HTML style attribute

Import: import {ActionBar} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation

code_examples:
import {ActionBar} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';

<ActionBar aria-label="Actions bar">
  <ActionBar.Section>
    <ActionBar.Group>
      <ActionBar.Item>
        <LegacyBreadcrumbs items={[{text: 'Root Item', action() {}}]} />
      </ActionBar.Item>
    </ActionBar.Group>
    <ActionBar.Group pull="right">
      <ActionBar.Item>
        <Button>Do something</Button>
      </ActionBar.Item>
    </ActionBar.Group>
  </ActionBar.Section>
</ActionBar>
content:
import {ActionBar} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';

<ActionBar aria-label="Actions bar">
  <ActionBar.Section>
    <ActionBar.Group>
      <ActionBar.Item>
        <LegacyBreadcrumbs items={[{text: 'Root Item', action() {}}]} />
      </ActionBar.Item>
    </ActionBar.Group>
    <ActionBar.Group pull="right">
      <ActionBar.Item>
        <Button>Do something</Button>
      </ActionBar.Item>
    </ActionBar.Group>
  </ActionBar.Section>
</ActionBar>

AsideHeader provides a flexible and customizable navigation experience within your application. Users can easily customize the appearance of the sidebar to match their branding colors and add personalized links and icons.

Props for AsideHeader:

- pinned (required): boolean — Navigation visual state. When true, the navigation is expanded (pinned open). When false, it is collapsed
- className: string — Additional CSS class
- collapseButtonWrapper: (defaultButton: React.ReactNode, data: {isExpanded: boolean; onChangePinned?: (pinned: boolean) => void}) => React.ReactNode — Custom wrapper for collapse button
- collapseTitle: string — Collapse button tooltip title
- customBackground: React.ReactNode — Custom background content
- customBackgroundClassName: string — Custom background CSS class
- defaultMenuGroups: MenuGroup[] — Default menu groups
- defaultMenuItems: AsideHeaderItem[] — Default menu items
- editMenuProps: EditMenuProps — Edit menu configuration props
- expandTitle: string — Expand button tooltip title
- headerDecoration: boolean — Navigation highlights top section with Logo and Subheader items
- hideCollapseButton: boolean — Hide collapse button
- isCompactMode: boolean — When true, menu items use compact height
- logo: LogoProps — Logo configuration
- menuGroups: MenuGroup[] — Menu groups
- menuItems: AsideHeaderItem[] — Main menu items
- menuMoreTitle: string — More menu items tooltip title
- onChangePinned: (pinned: boolean) => void — Pinned state change handler
- onMenuItemsChanged: (items: AsideHeaderItem[]) => void — Menu items changed callback
- panelItems: PanelItemProps[] — Panel items configuration
- renderContent: RenderContentType — Render function for main content area
- renderFooter: (data: {size: number; isExpanded: boolean; isPinned: boolean; asideRef: React.RefObject<HTMLDivElement>}) => React.ReactNode | React.ReactNode[] — Render function for the footer section
- subheaderItems: AsideHeaderItem[] — Subheader items placed below the logo
- topAlert: TopAlertProps — Top alert configuration

Import: import {AsideHeader} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation
Content component from the navigation library.

Props for Content:

- size (required): number
- className: string
- cssSizeVariableName: string
- renderContent: RenderContentType
- children: React.ReactNode

Import: import {Content} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation
The page footer component for desktop. Use Footer for the desktop version and MobileFooter for the mobile version.

Props for Footer:

- className: string — Footer class name
- copyright: string — The copyright text
- logo: LogoProps — The service logo properties
- logoWrapperClassName: string — The logo wrapper class name
- menuItems: FooterMenuItem[] — List of footer menu items
- moreButtonTitle: string — The more items button title
- onMoreButtonClick: MouseEventHandler<HTMLElement> — The more button click handler
- view: 'normal' | 'clear' — The footer view
- withDivider: boolean — Show top border on the footer

Import: import {Footer} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation

code_examples:
<Footer
  className="page-footer"
  copyright={`@ ${new Date().getFullYear()} My Service`}
  logo={{icon: logoIcon, iconSize: 24, text: 'My Service'}}
  menuItems={[
    {text: 'About', href: 'https://gravity-ui.com/', target: 'blank'},
    {text: 'Docs', href: 'https://gravity-ui.com/', target: 'blank'},
  ]}
/>
content:
<Footer
  className="page-footer"
  copyright={`@ ${new Date().getFullYear()} My Service`}
  logo={{icon: logoIcon, iconSize: 24, text: 'My Service'}}
  menuItems={[
    {text: 'About', href: 'https://gravity-ui.com/', target: 'blank'},
    {text: 'Docs', href: 'https://gravity-ui.com/', target: 'blank'},
  ]}
/>
A navigation panel for hotkeys documentation. The panel displays a set of hotkeys for your application with a description of their purpose.

Props for HotkeysPanel:

- hotkeys (required): Array — List of hotkey groups
- open (required): boolean — Whether drawer open or not
- className: string — Drawer class name
- container: HTMLElement — DOM element to render the drawer into
- drawerItemClassName: string — Drawer item class name
- emptyState: React.ReactNode — No search results placeholder
- filterClassName: string — Search input class name
- filterPlaceholder: string — Search input placeholder
- filterable: boolean — default: true — Whether show search input or not
- hideVeil: boolean — Whether to hide the drawer overlay (veil)
- itemContentClassName: string — List item content class name
- leftOffset: number | string — default: 0 — Drawer left offset
- listClassName: string — List class name
- onClose: () => void — Close drawer handler
- platform: 'pc' | 'mac' — Platform for hotkey display format
- style: CSSProperties — Custom styles for the drawer
- title: React.ReactNode — The panel title
- titleClassName: string — Title class name
- togglePanelHotkey: string — The panel open hotkey
- topOffset: number | string — default: 0 — Drawer top offset

Import: import {HotkeysPanel} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation
Logo icon is wrapped in UIKit Button, text is wrapped in HTML tag a or div, when passing hasWrapper prop.

Props for Logo:

- aria-label: string — HTML aria-label attribute passed to tag a
- aria-labelledby: string — HTML aria-labelledby attribute passed to tag a
- buttonClassName: string — HTML class for UIKit Button
- buttonWrapperClassName: string — HTML class attribute for UIKit Button wrapper
- className: string — HTML class attribute
- href: string — HTML href attribute
- icon: IconProps['data'] — Logo icon data
- iconClassName: string — HTML class attribute of the icon container
- iconSize: number — default: 24 — Width and height attribute
- iconSrc: string — HTML src attribute of the tag img
- onClick: (event: React.MouseEvent<HTMLElement, MouseEvent>) => void — Callback function called when the user clicks the Logo
- pinned: boolean — Logo view state: true (pinned) shows icon and text, false — icon only
- target: HTMLAttributeAnchorTarget — default: _self — HTML target attribute of the tag a
- text: (() => React.ReactNode) | string — Text displays only when pinned prop is true
- textSize: number — default: 15 — Text font size
- wrapper: (node: React.ReactNode, isExpanded: boolean) => React.ReactNode — Wrapper function. Wrapping icon and text in two separated containers

Import: import {Logo} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation
Header for Mobile navigation. MobileHeader provides maintaining Panels except for Modals.

Props for MobileHeader:

- className: string — Additional CSS class
- logo: LogoProps — Logo configuration
- menuItems: MobileMenuItem[] — Menu items in burger menu
- panelItems: ModalItem[] — Panel items

Import: import {MobileHeader} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation
MobileLogo component from the navigation library.

Props for MobileLogo:

- isExpanded (required): boolean

Import: import {MobileLogo} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation

The Settings component provides layouting functionality of settings panel with features including single-level and two-level grouping of items, filtering of the panel's content by headers of items, and displaying of loading status and content.

Props for Settings:

- children (required): React.ReactNode — Settings pages and groups
- emptyPlaceholder: string — Empty search results placeholder text
- filterPlaceholder: string — Search input placeholder
- initialPage: string — Initially selected page ID
- initialSearch: string — Initial search query
- loading: boolean — Flag of loading status
- onClose: () => void — Close handler
- onPageChange: (page: string | undefined) => void — Page change handler
- renderLoading: () => React.ReactNode — Custom loading render function
- renderNotFound: () => React.ReactNode — Custom not found render function
- renderRightAdornment: (item: SettingsItemProps) => React.ReactNode — Render function for right adornment per item
- selection: SettingsSelection — Selection configuration
- showRightAdornmentOnHover: boolean — Show right adornment only on hover
- title: string — Settings panel title
- view: 'normal' | 'mobile' — Settings view mode

Import: import {Settings} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation

<Settings>
  <Settings.Page title="Appearance">...</Settings.Page>
  <Settings.Page title="Notifications">...</Settings.Page>
</Settings>

<Settings>
  <Settings.Group groupTitle="My service">
    <Settings.Page title="Appearance">...</Settings.Page>
  </Settings.Group>
</Settings>
TopAlert component from the navigation library.
TopAlert has no documented props.

Import: import {TopAlert} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';
Package: @gravity-ui/navigation
